spec_match.rationale: The catalog provides an exceptionally clear reference for the CSS Conditional Level 5 registry, separating the currently shipped feature from proposed ones.

interactive.rationale: Features real-time filtering, keyphrase searching, dynamic percentage bar updates, and a fully-functional custom string probe.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent description, highlighting how the registry limits browser-private names and ensures interoperable checks.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes multiple proposed and fake entries that are expected to fail in current engines, effectively teaching registry boundary behavior.

references.rationale: Accurately references the ChromeStatus link and points directly to the section on named-features in the CSSWG editor draft.

design_system.rationale: Applies clear design system tokens, custom badges, and neat layout borders while maintaining AA accessibility.

openQuestions.title: Link proposed features to their respective CSSWG standard tracking issues
openQuestions.detail: The catalog lists proposed registry items such as timeline-scope and cross-document transitions, but they are static entries. Adding external links to their active CSS Working Group GitHub issues or W3C specs would allow developers to follow and participate in the stabilization of these future names.
